jokes.text: D'oh!
jokes.setup: Opening scene presumably shows Homer in some mishap
jokes.punchline: His signature frustrated exclamation
jokes.explanation: Homer's iconic catchphrase - perfectly economical character comedy that became cultural shorthand

jokes.text: I promised you a surprise today... We're going to take a test
jokes.setup: Teacher builds anticipation for a 'surprise'
jokes.punchline: The 'surprise' is mundane schoolwork
jokes.explanation: Classic misdirection joke - functional but predictable

jokes.text: Career Aptitude Normalizing Test-- Or 'CAN'T.'
jokes.setup: Official-sounding test name
jokes.punchline: Acronym spells out pessimistic 'CAN'T'
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that subtly undermines the test's purpose - typical Simpsons institutional satire

jokes.text: In spite of your masters from Bryn Mawr... you might end up a baby-sitter to dead-eyed fourth graders...
jokes.setup: Teacher explaining test might reveal harsh realities
jokes.punchline: She's describing her own disappointing career trajectory
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-joke where the teacher unknowingly describes herself - combines self-awareness with pathos

jokes.text: while your husband runs naked on a beach... with your marriage counselor!
jokes.setup: Continuing her bitter life disappointment speech
jokes.punchline: Escalates to absurdly specific marital betrayal
jokes.explanation: Good escalation that makes the teacher's bitterness comedically specific rather than generic

jokes.text: If I could be any animal, I would be: A) a carpenter ant... B) a nurse shark... or, C) a lawyer bird
jokes.setup: Standard multiple choice question format
jokes.punchline: All options are job titles awkwardly attached to animals
jokes.explanation: Solid absurdist joke satirizing career aptitude tests, though somewhat predictable

jokes.text: I prefer the smell of: A) gasoline... B) french fries... or, C) bank customers
jokes.setup: Another test question about preferences
jokes.punchline: 'Bank customers' as a smell category
jokes.explanation: Continues the absurdist test questions theme - functional but less inspired than previous

jokes.text: Since we have 15 minutes until recess... please put down your pencils... and stare at the front of the room
jokes.setup: Teacher needs to fill time before recess
jokes.punchline: Solution is literally doing nothing productive
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures the absurdity of school bureaucracy with economical delivery

jokes.text: Salmon gutter?!
jokes.setup: Lisa receives her career test result
jokes.punchline: Absurdly specific and undesirable job
jokes.explanation: Lisa's horror at an absurdly specific career choice - good character reaction to institutional absurdity

jokes.text: Systems analyst, systems analyst, systems analyst. Systems analyst.
jokes.setup: Multiple students getting career results
jokes.punchline: Everyone gets the same generic result repeated
jokes.explanation: Satirizes how standardized tests often produce meaningless generic results

jokes.text: Homemaker? It's like a mommy.
jokes.setup: Lisa gets 'homemaker' as career result
jokes.punchline: Teacher's condescending explanation
jokes.explanation: Sets up Lisa's feminist awakening arc while showing adult condescension

jokes.text: Before I saw these test results, I had you pegged a drifter.
jokes.setup: Teacher commenting on Bart's police officer result
jokes.punchline: Casually admits she expected him to be homeless
jokes.explanation: Dark humor delivered deadpan - teacher's casual cruelty toward struggling student

jokes.text: Wow. A drifter. Lousy sheriff. Run me out of town. He's lost my vote.
jokes.setup: Bart processing being called a potential drifter
jokes.punchline: Immediately adopts drifter persona and Old West grievances
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart response - immediately romanticizes being an outcast with kid logic

jokes.text: I might as well be dead.
jokes.setup: Lisa's reaction to homemaker career result
jokes.punchline: Dramatic overreaction typical of precocious kid
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Lisa's intellectual ambitions and dramatic tendencies

jokes.text: The police said I was too dumb.
jokes.setup: Homer explaining why he didn't become a policeman
jokes.punchline: Matter-of-fact admission of being rejected for stupidity
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer - completely unashamed self-awareness of his limitations

jokes.text: I'll be unappreciated in my own country... but my gutsy blues stylings will electrify the French.
jokes.setup: Lisa fantasizing about being a jazz musician
jokes.punchline: Detailed romantic vision of artistic martyrdom
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lisa - intellectualizing artistic dreams with precocious vocabulary

jokes.text: I may or may not die young. I haven't decided.
jokes.setup: Lisa continuing her jazz musician fantasy
jokes.punchline: Treating death as a career choice to be decided
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion - treats the romantic 'die young' trope as a practical decision, perfectly capturing Lisa's analytical nature

jokes.text: Isn't anybody gonna follow in my footsteps?
jokes.setup: Homer wanting his kids to emulate him
jokes.punchline: Given his established incompetence, this is clearly undesirable
jokes.explanation: Homer's obliviousness to why his children might want different paths

jokes.text: Oh, Lord, no.
jokes.setup: Asked if Lisa could be a professional musician
jokes.punchline: Immediate, emphatic rejection
jokes.explanation: Perfect comedic timing - the blunt honesty after pretending to be encouraging

jokes.text: You've inherited a finger condition... known as stubbiness. It usually comes... from the father's side.
jokes.setup: Musician explaining Lisa's musical limitations
jokes.punchline: Treats 'stubbiness' as a hereditary medical condition
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist joke that medicalizes a common physical trait with mock-serious delivery

jokes.text: D'oh! Stupid fingers.
jokes.setup: Homer realizing he passed on stubby fingers
jokes.punchline: His signature exclamation plus self-blame
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Homer's catchphrase with appropriate self-blame for genetic inheritance

jokes.text: My God, they are stubby.
jokes.setup: Lisa looking at her fingers after passionate speech
jokes.punchline: Deflating realization that undermines her argument
jokes.explanation: Good comedic deflation - Lisa's intellectual argument crumbles when faced with physical reality

jokes.text: I didn't steal that copper wire. I just thought they were throwing it out.
jokes.setup: Police arriving at a house
jokes.punchline: Criminal immediately confesses to unrelated crime
jokes.explanation: Classic criminal behavior - guilty conscience makes him confess to wrong crime

jokes.text: It's called a baton, son. What's it for? We club people... with it.
jokes.setup: Bart asking about police equipment
jokes.punchline: Brutally honest explanation of police violence
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about police brutality delivered matter-of-factly to a child

jokes.text: You run red lights, park wherever you please... hot-and-cold-running chicks.
jokes.setup: Eddie describing perks of being a cop
jokes.punchline: Escalates from minor privileges to sexist fantasy
jokes.explanation: Shows corrupt cop mentality with period-typical casual sexism

jokes.text: Tonight his honor is... polling the electorate.
jokes.setup: Cop explaining where Mayor Quimby is
jokes.punchline: Euphemism for sexual encounter
jokes.explanation: Political corruption joke using euphemistic language - solid but not exceptional

jokes.text: How would you like a street named after you?
jokes.setup: Mayor Quimby talking to a woman
jokes.punchline: Using political power as pickup line
jokes.explanation: Characterizes corrupt politician using power for personal gain - competent character humor

jokes.text: Dear log... this will be my last entry... for you were a journal of my hopes and dreams... and now, I have none.
jokes.setup: Lisa dramatically addressing her diary
jokes.punchline: Calls it 'log' and delivers overwrought farewell
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lisa melodrama - intellectual vocabulary applied to childhood disappointment

jokes.text: They used nylon rope this time. It feels so smooth my skin-- almost sensuous.
jokes.setup: Criminal being tied up by police
jokes.punchline: Finds the experience sexually pleasurable
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about criminal's masochistic tendencies - pushes boundaries appropriately for character

jokes.text: Good thing this alley got so narrow in the middle.
jokes.setup: Criminal chase sequence ends with capture
jokes.punchline: Explains capture through convenient architectural feature
jokes.explanation: Acknowledges the convenient coincidence of the visual gag with matter-of-fact delivery

jokes.text: He's unconscious, sir. Oh, they can still hear things.
jokes.setup: Lou pointing out criminal is unconscious
jokes.punchline: Wiggum's nonsensical belief about unconscious people
jokes.explanation: Good Wiggum stupidity - confidently wrong about basic medical facts

jokes.text: This morning I turned bacon, eggs, and toast... into a smiley face for Bart and Homer... What's the point? They'll never notice.
jokes.setup: Marge explaining creative homemaking to Lisa
jokes.punchline: Lisa's cynical assessment that effort goes unappreciated
jokes.explanation: Good character interaction showing Lisa's cynicism vs. Marge's optimism about domestic life

jokes.text: Hmm, umm, hmm...
jokes.setup: Homer and Bart eating the smiley face breakfast
jokes.punchline: They indeed don't notice the creative presentation
jokes.explanation: Perfect payoff to Lisa's prediction - visual confirmation that they're oblivious to Marge's effort

jokes.text: take a look at these surveillance photos.
jokes.setup: Bart defending himself from blame
jokes.punchline: Has actual surveillance evidence like a detective
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Bart's new police-influenced behavior in domestic setting

jokes.text: You know what I want to be? The girl on the oatmeal box?
jokes.setup: Student sharing career aspiration
jokes.punchline: Wants to be anonymous advertising image
jokes.explanation: Absurdist child logic - wanting to be a corporate mascot as life goal

jokes.text: Women can't be astronauts... They distract the men... so they wouldn't keep their minds on the road.
jokes.setup: Lisa wanting to be an astronaut
jokes.punchline: Nelson's sexist logic mixed with car driving metaphor
jokes.explanation: Shows period-typical sexism through child character's mixed-up reasoning

jokes.text: we'll all live... in cities on the moon... I was wrong about that
jokes.setup: Marge encouraging Lisa about women's progress
jokes.punchline: Admits she was wrong about moon cities prediction
jokes.explanation: Marge's earnest but misguided optimism about technological progress

jokes.text: Sleek, vigilant puma-- principal of the mountains.
jokes.setup: Skinner admiring what's presumably a puma statue/image
jokes.punchline: Overwrought poetic description with pretentious vocabulary
jokes.explanation: Perfect Skinner pretension - applying grandiose language to mundane school decoration

jokes.text: That's a bad-girl bathroom!
jokes.setup: Lisa going into wrong bathroom
jokes.punchline: Elementary school social hierarchy based on bathroom choice
jokes.explanation: Good observation about elementary school social dynamics and territorial behavior

jokes.text: you really have to wonder at the mentality... that would desecrate a helpless puma.
jokes.setup: Skinner discovering vandalism of his beloved puma
jokes.punchline: Treats inanimate object as victim of violence
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to earlier puma worship - Skinner's melodramatic reaction to vandalism

jokes.text: Bart Simpson on the side of law and order?! Has the world gone topsy-turvy?
jokes.setup: Skinner learning Bart reported Willie
jokes.punchline: Theatrical shock at role reversal
jokes.explanation: Acknowledges the irony of Bart becoming authority figure - functional character moment

jokes.text: Let's can the euphemisms. How would you like to be a hall monitor?
jokes.setup: Skinner offering Bart a position
jokes.punchline: Calls hall monitor work 'euphemisms' while offering it
jokes.explanation: Skinner's blunt honesty about what hall monitoring really entails - institutional corruption

jokes.text: Ay, caramba.!
jokes.setup: Bart watching courthouse testimony about murder
jokes.punchline: His signature exclamation at serious criminal proceedings
jokes.explanation: Bart's catchphrase used appropriately for dramatic situation - good character consistency

jokes.text: You got yourself a narc.
jokes.setup: Skinner offering hall monitor position
jokes.punchline: Bart accepting with criminal terminology
jokes.explanation: Shows Bart understanding the moral ambiguity of his new role using street language

jokes.text: Oh, we always have one good kid and one lousy kid.
jokes.setup: Marge noting Bart's grades up, Lisa's down
jokes.punchline: Homer's casual acceptance of having one failure
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer parenting philosophy - low expectations delivered matter-of-factly

jokes.text: Shove it.
jokes.setup: Teacher telling Lisa to sprinkle sparkles
jokes.punchline: Lisa's uncharacteristic rudeness
jokes.explanation: Perfect character reversal - Lisa's rebellion expressed in efficient, shocking rudeness

jokes.text: What do you got?
jokes.setup: Asked what she's rebelling against
jokes.punchline: Classic rebellious teen response from elementary schooler
jokes.explanation: References classic 'Wild One' movie quote but applied to Lisa's situation

jokes.text: I'll smoke it in class.
jokes.setup: Bad girl offering Lisa a cigarette
jokes.punchline: Lisa's amateur attempt at being rebellious
jokes.explanation: Shows Lisa's inexperience with rebellion - escalates in wrong direction with innocent logic

jokes.text: Madre de Dios.! The legends were true!
jokes.setup: Bart seeing confiscated items room
jokes.punchline: Treats mundane storage as mythical discovery
jokes.explanation: Good escalation treating school storage room like archaeological discovery

jokes.text: and this plastic derriere.
jokes.setup: Skinner listing confiscated items
jokes.punchline: Uses formal term for plastic butt
jokes.explanation: Typical Skinner pretension - formal vocabulary for juvenile toy

jokes.text: Notice the identical elongated loops on the D's. Forgery!
jokes.setup: Bart analyzing Nelson's absence slip
jokes.punchline: Professional handwriting analysis by elementary student
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Bart's new investigative skills applied to school bureaucracy

jokes.text: So he didn't have leprosy.
jokes.setup: Proving Nelson's absence excuse was forged
jokes.punchline: Reveals the absurd medical excuse Nelson used
jokes.explanation: Absurdist humor - elementary student claiming medieval disease to skip school

jokes.text: There will be no mockery of your name, Mr. Glasscock.
jokes.setup: Skinner addressing substitute teacher
jokes.punchline: Draws attention to embarrassing surname while claiming protection
jokes.explanation: Classic ironic protection - mentioning the thing you're trying to prevent

jokes.text: the Battle of New Orleans. I mean... Andrew Jackson.
jokes.setup: Teacher trying to answer question without teacher's edition
jokes.punchline: Gives battle name instead of person, then corrects awkwardly
jokes.explanation: Shows teacher's incompetence without answer key - confuses related historical facts

jokes.text: Well, you're earning your 18 grand a year.
jokes.setup: Teacher making mistake in class
jokes.punchline: Lisa's cutting assessment of teacher salary vs. performance
jokes.explanation: Sharp social commentary delivered with Lisa's new cynical edge - economic reality of teaching

jokes.text: Does anyone know... the multiplication table? Please, don't panic. They can smell fear.
jokes.setup: School in crisis without teacher's editions
jokes.punchline: Treats students like dangerous animals
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation showing institutional panic - students become predators to fear

jokes.text: Have I ever told you kids about the '60s?
jokes.setup: Overwhelmed substitute trying to teach
jokes.punchline: Defaults to personal anecdotes instead of curriculum
jokes.explanation: Common substitute teacher trope - avoiding real teaching with personal stories

jokes.text: Baa!
jokes.setup: Police search with dogs to find stolen books
jokes.punchline: They find sheep in a locker instead
jokes.explanation: Simple absurdist visual gag - sheep in school locker is random but not particularly clever or meaningful

jokes.text: Simpson, you've just saved this school $120.
jokes.setup: Books are found and crisis resolved
jokes.punchline: Celebrates absurdly small savings amount
jokes.explanation: Shows school's pathetic budget constraints - $120 is treated as significant savings

jokes.text: 400 days. I can do that standing on my head. All right, 500 days. Ooh! Big man! 600 days.
jokes.setup: Skinner sentencing Bart to detention
jokes.punchline: Bart's sarcasm keeps increasing his punishment
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Bart's inability to quit while ahead - classic self-defeating behavior

jokes.text: when you do... I'll be right there to borrow money.
jokes.setup: Bart explaining why he protected Lisa's future
jokes.punchline: Reveals selfish motivation behind noble gesture
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart moment - subverts apparent selflessness with characteristic self-interest, but in an endearing way
